summary: Backend-only testing of driver scenario completed. 20/20 tests passed for the API layer, but discovered CRITICAL BUG: home location fields (home_latitude, home_longitude, home_address) are not saved by backend despite frontend requiring them as mandatory.

critical.issue: Backend DeliverySettingsUpdate schema missing home_address, home_latitude, home_longitude fields. API accepts these fields but silently ignores them - data is NOT persisted. Frontend requires mandatory location on line 82-84 of DeliverySettingsTab.js
critical.root_cause: DeliverySettingsUpdate Pydantic model in auth.py (line 539-543) only has: vehicle_type, vehicle_number, working_city, working_hours - missing home location fields
critical.suggested_fix: Add home_address: Optional[str], home_latitude: Optional[float], home_longitude: Optional[float] to DeliverySettingsUpdate model and include them in update_delivery_settings handler

integration_issues.issue: Frontend requires mandatory home location (line 82-84) but backend doesn't save these fields - user sees success toast but data is lost on reload

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix DeliverySettingsUpdate model in /app/backend/routes/auth.py to include home_address, home_latitude, home_longitude fields
Update update_delivery_settings endpoint to save these fields to database
Update get_delivery_settings endpoint to return these fields from database
critical_code_review_comments: DeliverySettingsUpdate model (auth.py line 539) is incomplete - missing home location fields that frontend sends
Frontend enforces mandatory location but backend silently discards the data - critical data loss bug
Driver registration correctly sets is_approved=False for new drivers
Admin approval workflow works correctly via /api/admin/delivery/{id}/approve
Payment accounts CRUD working correctly for delivery users

success_rate.backend: 100% - 20/20 API tests passed (but home location persistence bug found through manual verification)

test_driver_created.note: New driver created each test run with random phone

seed_data_creation: TEST_سائق اختباري جديد driver created, documents submitted, and approved during test
context_for_next_testing_agent: Driver scenario (Part 2 of 3) complete but with CRITICAL BUG. Home location fields (home_latitude, home_longitude, home_address) are NOT persisted by backend despite frontend requiring them as mandatory. All other driver APIs working correctly (registration, approval, settings, payment accounts, availability).

driver_registration.notes: New driver with user_type='delivery' gets is_approved=False by default

pending_drivers_list.notes: Returns drivers with pending document status

driver_documents_submit.notes: Driver can submit required documents (national_id, photos, license)

driver_approval.notes: Sets is_approved=True on user and documents

driver_login_after_approval.notes: Driver can login and is_approved=True confirmed

driver_settings_get.notes: Returns vehicle_type, vehicle_number, working_city, working_hours (NO home location fields)

driver_settings_home_location.notes: API accepts home_latitude, home_longitude, home_address but DOES NOT save them - returns success but data is lost

payment_accounts_crud.notes: Full CRUD operations working for shamcash account type

driver_availability.notes: Can get and set availability status

unauthorized_access.notes: 401 for unauthenticated, 403 for wrong user type

rca_of_the_issue.bug_title: Driver home location not persisted by backend
reproduction_steps: 5. home_* fields are missing from response - data was not saved
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: DeliverySettingsUpdate Pydantic model in /app/backend/routes/auth.py (line 539-543) doesn't include home_address, home_latitude, home_longitude fields. The update_delivery_settings handler only processes fields defined in the model, so home location data is silently ignored.
rca_of_the_issue.impact: Critical - Driver cannot save their home location despite frontend requiring it as mandatory. User experience: sees success message but data is lost on page reload.
rca_of_the_issue.suggested_mitigation: Update DeliverySettingsUpdate model to include: home_address: Optional[str] = None, home_latitude: Optional[float] = None, home_longitude: Optional[float] = None. Then update the update_delivery_settings and get_delivery_settings functions to handle these fields.
